title: ChildCareComplaint
description: A complaint or concern about a CHILD CARE PROVIDER / program (health, safety, staffing, licensing, or quality) — NOT a report of child abuse or neglect and NOT case data. NET-NEW: there is NO ACS-owned API today; provider complaints are routed to NYC 311 (portal.311.nyc.gov) and licensing complaints ultimately to the regulator, while suspected abuse/neglect of a child goes to the NY State OCFS Statewide Central Register (1-800-342-3720), never through this surface. This schema serves both the read surface (a submitter's own complaints) and the write surface (submit a provider complaint). It deliberately models the provider concern, not the child.

complaintId.description: Server-assigned complaint / 311 service-request number. Omit on create; returned on the created resource.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Set by ACS/311, not the submitter.

providerName.description: Name of the child care program / provider the complaint is about.

providerAddress.description: Address of the provider, if known.

description.description: Free-text description of the concern with the provider.

childInvolved.description: Whether a specific child is at immediate risk. IMPORTANT: if a child is being abused or neglected this is NOT the right channel — the submitter must be redirected to the NY State Central Register (1-800-342-3720). This flag exists to trigger that redirect, not to collect case data.

routedTo.description: Where the complaint was routed for handling.
